evals.prompt: I can't tell if this subscription state machine handles retries and cancellations correctly. Build a prototype to explore it.
evals.expected_output: The agent chooses the logic prototype branch, builds a tiny interactive terminal prototype close to the relevant module, and surfaces full state after each action.
assertions: Identifies the question the prototype must answer.
Chooses the logic/state branch rather than a UI route.
Creates clearly marked throwaway code close to the relevant production context.
Provides one command to run the prototype.
Prints the relevant state after each action.

evals.prompt: We need to see three very different directions for the new onboarding screen before deciding. Make a throwaway prototype.
evals.expected_output: The agent chooses the UI prototype branch, creates switchable route variations using existing routing conventions, and records the question and cleanup path.
assertions: Provides several radically different switchable variations.
Uses a URL search parameter and floating bottom bar when the project routing supports it.
Marks the code as throwaway and avoids production polish.
Captures the answer or writes NOTES.md with cleanup needed when done.
